content: Issue type: billing. Symptom: customer reports duplicate subscription charge in the same billing cycle. Resolution: verify invoice IDs and payment intents, confirm whether one charge is pending authorization hold, and if true duplicate create refund for the extra charge. Expected timeline: bank-visible reversal in 3 to 5 business days for cards. Response guidance: apologize, confirm amount, provide refund reference, and set expectation on settlement delay.

Issue type: billing. Symptom: invoice total does not match expected monthly rate after plan change. Cause: proration from mid-cycle upgrade or downgrade. Resolution steps: calculate unused credit from prior plan, apply prorated cost of new plan for remaining days, and attach invoice line-item explanation. If mismatch remains greater than one cent due to tax rounding, open billing adjustment ticket.

Issue type: billing. Symptom: payment failed and account moved toward grace state. Troubleshooting: check gateway decline code, verify card expiration and postal code, ask customer to retry with same method once and alternate method once, then trigger manual retry from billing admin panel. Escalate if repeated soft declines continue beyond 24 hours for enterprise accounts.

Issue type: billing policy. Standard policy: unused subscription time may be credited on cancellation where contract allows; one-time usage charges are non-refundable unless service defect confirmed. Agent action: avoid promising exceptions without policy check. Escalation criteria: legal terms dispute, chargeback already filed, or high-value annual contracts.

Issue type: billing. Symptom: customer cannot find current invoice. Checks: verify billing cycle close date, confirm invoice job completed, and validate customer has correct tenant and organization scope. Workaround: generate manual invoice PDF if ledger posted but document generation failed. If ledger not posted, escalate to finance operations.

Issue type: technical-auth. Symptom: password reset email not received. Steps: validate account email, check spam and blocked senders, verify suppression list status, resend reset token, and provide direct reset link fallback when policy permits. Escalate if mail provider rejects with hard bounce or user identity cannot be verified.

Issue type: technical-auth. Symptom: customer logs in successfully but returns to login page. Typical causes: expired session cookie, clock skew, third-party cookie blocking, or stale frontend bundle. Fix: clear site cookies, hard refresh, verify system time, and retest in incognito. If reproducible across devices, inspect auth callback URL and token audience configuration.

Issue type: technical-auth high severity when admin is locked out. Recovery procedure: verify identity with secondary factor on file, issue temporary recovery code valid for 15 minutes, force MFA re-enrollment, and invalidate all active sessions. Escalate immediately for suspected account takeover indicators.

Issue type: technical-api. Symptom: API returns 401 unauthorized. Debug checklist: confirm key exists, key not expired, correct header name, and request sent to expected environment endpoint. For Azure OpenAI and Search integrations ensure resource endpoint and key are from same resource. Rotate key if leaked in logs or screenshots.

Issue type: technical-api. Symptom: API returns 403 forbidden despite valid authentication. Causes: role assignment missing, resource firewall restrictions, or SKU feature not enabled. Resolution: verify principal permissions, check IP allowlist or private endpoint policy, and validate operation supported by current tier.

Issue type: reliability. Symptom: intermittent 429 throttling. Mitigation: implement exponential backoff with jitter, reduce burst concurrency, cache repeated KB queries, and trim prompt/token size. For persistent throttling evaluate higher quota or move traffic to additional region. User messaging should state temporary load and retry window.

Issue type: reliability. Symptom: upstream model or dependency returns 5xx. Procedure: capture correlation ID and timestamp, retry idempotent requests up to configured limit, and degrade gracefully with fallback response. Escalate to platform team when error rate exceeds alert threshold for more than 5 minutes.

Feature area: knowledge base ingestion. Expected format is JSONL with one JSON object per line. Required fields for current script: id and content; text also accepted as fallback for content. Common failures: invalid JSON syntax, blank content, duplicate IDs causing replace behavior. Validation step: parse file locally before upload.

Deployment and local run guidance: from backend directory use command uv run python -m support.tools.kb_search --ingest ./data/knowledge_base.jsonl. Ensure working directory includes src package in project context and file path exists. If using .data folder for authoring, copy or symlink to ./data before ingestion.

Issue type: technical-kb. Symptom: search returns empty chunk list. Checks: verify AZURE_SEARCH_ENDPOINT, AZURE_SEARCH_KEY, index name, and that index actually has documents. Confirm query text is non-empty after preprocessing. Run targeted probe query with known keyword from ingested content. Re-ingest if document count is zero.

Issue type: quality. Symptom: retrieval returns unrelated chunks. Improvements: enrich content with domain-specific terms, split long entries into focused chunks, include product and error keywords, and tune top_k. Add explicit troubleshooting phrases customers use so keyword search can match natural language reports.

Configuration issue: application fails at startup due to missing environment variables. Required baseline includes AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT, AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY, AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT, AZURE_SEARCH_ENDPOINT, AZURE_SEARCH_KEY, AZURE_SEARCH_INDEX, and Cosmos values when persistence enabled. Use .env.example as source of truth and fail fast with clear validation message.

Configuration issue: model call fails because deployment name is incorrect. Symptoms include not found or bad request from Azure OpenAI. Resolution: confirm deployment name exactly matches portal deployment, verify API version compatibility, and separate chat deployment from embedding deployment names.

Configuration issue: cannot connect to Cosmos DB. Checks: endpoint format must include https and documents domain, key must be current, database and container names must exist, and partition key path should align with conversation_id usage. For network restricted accounts verify VNet and firewall rules permit app egress.

Deployment challenge: container app starts then crashes. Debug path: inspect container logs for import/module errors, verify start command uv run uvicorn src.support.main:app --reload not used in production, ensure required env vars are injected, and check health probe path and port match service configuration.

Deployment challenge: new release not visible after deploy. Cause often stale image tag or wrong registry reference. Action: build with explicit immutable tag, update container app to same tag, and confirm rollout revision is active. Avoid reusing latest without digest tracking in production pipelines.

Infrastructure challenge: Bicep deployment fails with policy or quota errors. Procedure: review deployment operation details, identify exact failing resource, adjust location or SKU to available quota, and rerun incremental deployment. For OpenAI model capacity issues select alternate region or reduce requested capacity.

Feature behavior: feedback evaluator may route conversation for retry when feedback_signal is not_helpful and retry_count is below MAX_RETRY_COUNT. Guardrail: once retry limit reached, route to escalation to avoid infinite loop. Debug by logging retry_count transitions and edge decision outputs.

Feature behavior: high severity incidents should escalate to human support when automated steps are unsafe or insufficient. Examples include production outage, suspected security incident, or data loss. Agent response should acknowledge urgency, provide immediate containment checks, and create escalation ticket with conversation context.

Technical issue: frontend does not receive streamed assistant tokens. Verify WebSocket endpoint path, CORS and origin settings, proxy timeout, and whether backend event loop is blocked by synchronous code. Use browser network tab and backend logs to confirm connection open, messages sent, and clean close frame.

Debugging procedure: when incidents occur, query Application Insights by conversation_id and node_name to reconstruct graph path and latency per node. Check spikes in token usage and retrieval latency. If no telemetry appears, verify APPINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING and SDK initialization during startup.

Debugging playbook for startup errors: run uv sync, then uv run python -m support.tools.kb_search --help to verify imports, then uv run uvicorn src.support.main:app --port 8003. If import fails, inspect PYTHONPATH and package layout under src. Fix missing dependencies in pyproject and re-sync.

Debugging playbook for ingestion errors: confirm JSONL path exists, open first 5 lines for JSON validity, verify Azure Search credentials, and test index creation permissions. Capture stack trace and correlation timestamps. Most common root causes are invalid key, wrong endpoint, or blocked network egress.

Security scenario: API key accidentally committed or shared. Immediate actions: revoke and rotate affected keys, purge exposed logs where possible, invalidate active sessions if auth tokens leaked, and document incident timeline. Update .gitignore and secret scanning to prevent recurrence.

Common user interaction: customer asks for ticket or issue status. Agent behavior: confirm ticket ID, summarize latest known state, provide next milestone with expected time window, and offer escalation option if SLA window exceeded. Never fabricate status when backend state is unavailable.

Common user interaction: feature request submission. Workflow: acknowledge request, capture use case and business impact, classify as enhancement, and route to product backlog with priority hints from customer tier and urgency. Provide transparent expectation that request is reviewed, not guaranteed.

Common user interaction: how-to guidance such as changing plan, exporting invoices, updating profile, or enabling notifications. Agent should provide concise step-by-step instructions, include prerequisite roles or permissions, and link to related KB entries when available.

Escalation procedure: ticket should include conversation_id, user impact, issue category, severity, steps already attempted, relevant error messages, and timestamps. Missing context causes delayed resolution and duplicate customer prompts. Always attach top retrieved KB chunks and graph decision path for handoff clarity.

Testing guidance: include unit tests for classification, edge routing, retry loop, and escalation conditions. Include integration tests for KB retrieval and checkpointer persistence. For regressions, replay real anonymized conversations to ensure response quality and routing logic remain stable after prompt or code changes.

Performance concern: high token usage increases latency and cost. Mitigation: summarize prior conversation state, cap message history window, store structured state instead of full transcript where feasible, and avoid redundant system instructions. Monitor per-node token metrics and set budget alerts.

Pre-deployment checklist: environment variables set, Azure resources reachable, KB index populated, health endpoint passing, logging enabled, alert rules configured, and rollback plan validated. Run smoke test covering billing, technical, and general flows before promoting revision to production traffic.
